title: Process-heap allocation for BIF and NIF results — eliminate Box::leak in native boundary
purpose: BIF and NIF results that return boxed terms (tuples, lists, binaries, floats, maps) are allocated via Box::leak through ProcessContext's alloc_tuple/alloc_cons and direct Box::leak calls across 20+ native files. These allocations are permanent — never freed, never garbage collected. Every BIF call that returns a boxed term leaks memory. This brief routes all BIF/NIF boxed result allocation through the calling process's heap via ProcessContext, making them GC-reclaimable.
task: Give ProcessContext access to the process heap. Replace alloc_tuple and alloc_cons in context.rs to allocate on the process heap (with GC-safe ensure-space). Provide additional process-heap allocation helpers (alloc_float, alloc_binary, alloc_bigint, alloc_map). Migrate all BIF/NIF files that call Box::leak directly to use the new ProcessContext helpers. The meridian_ffi helpers (ok_binary, err_binary, ok_nil) are migrated too. Scope: native boundary allocation only. Does not touch interpreter literal path (that is B-050).

requirements.title: ProcessContext gains process heap access
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL give ProcessContext a mutable reference to the calling process's heap so that BIF/NIF results can be allocated on GC-managed memory. THE SYSTEM SHALL provide ensure_heap_space(words: usize) to trigger GC if insufficient space.
acceptance: ProcessContext gains a field or method to access the process heap (&mut [u64] or a HeapAllocator trait)
ensure_heap_space(n) checks available heap words, triggers minor GC if insufficient, triggers major GC if still insufficient
The heap reference is valid for the duration of the BIF call and points to the process's current heap
ProcessContext's existing fields (pid, registers, atom_table, etc.) are unchanged

requirements.title: Process-heap allocation helpers in ProcessContext
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL replace alloc_tuple and alloc_cons with process-heap-allocating versions. THE SYSTEM SHALL add alloc_float, alloc_binary, alloc_bigint, alloc_list, and alloc_map helpers. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT use Box::leak in any allocation helper.
acceptance: alloc_tuple(&mut self, elements: &[Term]) -> Result<Term, Term> allocates on process heap, calls ensure_heap_space first
alloc_cons(&mut self, head: Term, tail: Term) -> Result<Term, Term> allocates 2 words on process heap
alloc_float(&mut self, value: f64) -> Result<Term, Term> allocates header + f64 on process heap
alloc_binary(&mut self, bytes: &[u8]) -> Result<Term, Term> allocates header + length + data on process heap
alloc_bigint(&mut self, sign: bool, limbs: &[u64]) -> Result<Term, Term> allocates header + sign + limbs on process heap
alloc_list(&mut self, elements: &[Term]) -> Result<Term, Term> builds a proper list (chain of cons cells) on process heap
alloc_map(&mut self, keys: &[Term], values: &[Term]) -> Result<Term, Term> allocates flatmap on process heap
All helpers use write_float/write_tuple/write_cons/write_binary from term::boxed — same term layout as before
No Box::leak in context.rs

requirements.title: Migrate meridian_ffi helpers
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL replace ok_binary, err_binary, and ok_nil in meridian_ffi.rs with ProcessContext-based allocation. Each NIF SHALL use its _ctx parameter for allocation instead of Box::leak.
acceptance: ok_binary uses ctx.alloc_binary(bytes) instead of Box::leak
err_binary uses ctx.alloc_binary(bytes) instead of Box::leak
ok_nil returns a simple nil term (no allocation needed) or uses ctx.alloc_tuple for the {ok, nil} wrapper
All 6 meridian_ffi NIFs use ctx for any boxed allocation
No Box::leak in meridian_ffi.rs

requirements.title: Migrate gate3_bifs Box::leak sites
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL replace all Box::leak calls in gate3_bifs/ with ProcessContext allocation helpers.
acceptance: gate3_bifs/mod.rs: timeout_tuple and make_ref result allocation via ctx
gate3_bifs/type_conversion.rs: integer_to_list, list_to_binary, float_to_list, atom_to_binary via ctx
gate3_bifs/additional.rs: any remaining Box::leak sites via ctx
No Box::leak in any file under native/gate3_bifs/

requirements.title: Migrate stdlib_stubs Box::leak sites
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL replace all Box::leak calls in stdlib_stubs/ with ProcessContext allocation helpers.
acceptance: stdlib_stubs/mod.rs: rand float, BIF results via ctx
stdlib_stubs/string_bifs.rs: string operation results via ctx
stdlib_stubs/collection_bifs.rs: list/map results via ctx
stdlib_stubs/maps_bifs.rs: map construction via ctx
stdlib_stubs/math_bifs.rs: float results via ctx
stdlib_stubs/uri_bifs.rs: binary results via ctx
stdlib_stubs/encoding_bifs.rs: binary results via ctx
stdlib_stubs/io_bifs.rs: I/O results via ctx
stdlib_stubs/gleam_stdlib_ffi.rs: FFI results via ctx
stdlib_stubs/gleam_stdlib_ffi2.rs: FFI results via ctx
stdlib_stubs/type_conversion_bifs.rs: conversion results via ctx
stdlib_stubs/erlang_stubs.rs: erlang stub results via ctx
No Box::leak in any file under native/stdlib_stubs/

requirements.title: Migrate otp_stubs and bifs.rs Box::leak sites
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL replace all Box::leak calls in otp_stubs/ and native/bifs.rs with ProcessContext allocation helpers.
acceptance: native/bifs.rs: any remaining Box::leak (timeout_tuple_term, etc.) via ctx
otp_stubs/gleam_stubs.rs: results via ctx
otp_stubs/erlang_stubs.rs: results via ctx
No Box::leak in native/bifs.rs or any file under native/otp_stubs/

requirements.title: Migrate term/json.rs Box::leak sites
requirements.spec: WHEN JSON terms are constructed, THE SYSTEM SHALL allocate on the process heap instead of using Box::leak. THE SYSTEM SHALL thread ProcessContext (or a heap reference) through JSON-to-Term conversion.
acceptance: term/json.rs Box::leak sites at lines 310, 325, 332, 373, 399 replaced with process-heap allocation
JSON-to-Term conversion accepts a heap allocator parameter
No Box::leak in term/json.rs

requirements.title: Tests for process-heap BIF allocation
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL include tests verifying BIF results are on the process heap and reclaimable by GC.
acceptance: Test: a BIF returning a tuple (e.g. erlang:make_ref) — result is on the process heap, survives GC, is collected when unreachable
Test: a tight loop calling rand:uniform/0 1000 times — process heap grows and GC reclaims, no monotonic memory growth
Test: meridian_ffi:read_file result is on process heap, GC-reclaimable
Test: alloc_float, alloc_binary, alloc_list, alloc_map all produce valid terms on the process heap
All existing BIF tests pass — results are identical, only allocation location changed

boundaries: SHALL NOT touch interpreter literal allocation — that is B-050
SHALL NOT touch the atom-name intern Box::leak in atom/table.rs — that is legitimate and permanent by design
SHALL NOT change NativeFn signature — BIFs still receive &[Term] and &mut ProcessContext, return Result<Term, Term>
SHALL NOT change term layout or encoding — only where the memory backing the term lives
SHALL NOT implement a global term allocator or arena — allocation targets the calling process's heap specifically
verification: cargo test --workspace passes with all existing tests plus new heap-allocation tests
grep for 'Box::leak' in native/ returns zero matches (except tests if any)
grep for 'Box::leak' in term/json.rs returns zero matches
A BIF-heavy workload shows GC reclaiming BIF result memory — no monotonic growth
ProcessContext::alloc_* helpers all allocate on the process heap, not static/global memory
